title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Execution Trace: Residual Byte Boundary
summary: A body reader allocates from attacker Content-Length, treats premature EOF as success, consumes the next pipelined message, and reuses a connection after incomplete content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the residual byte boundary increment in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Residual Byte Boundary · Theory Residual Byte Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2508 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Residual Byte Boundary contract for the supplied data.
